title: Email Verification System
statement: Build a robust email verification system using Nodemailer. Implement APIs to send verification emails with unique tokens and verify user input tokens. The system should handle rate limiting, prevent brute-force attacks, and store email verification status in memory for this challenge.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array or mock database provided for email verification status
Rate limit should be enforced on email sending to prevent brute-force attacks

endpoints.description: Sends a verification email to the provided email address. The email contains a unique token that can be used to verify the email address. The API should respond with the token sent to the user and store the email verification status in memory.
Verifies the provided token and updates the email verification status in memory. The API should respond with the verification status.

starterCode.index.js: const express = require('express');const app = express();const nodemailer = require('nodemailer');const { RateLimiterMemory } = require('rate-limiter-flexible');const { nanoid } = require('nanoid');const emails = [];const limiter = new RateLimiterMemory({points: 10, duration: 1});app.use(express.json());const transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({host: "smtp.example.com", port: 587, auth: {user: "username", pass: "password"}});const getVerificationToken = () => {return nanoid(32);}const sendVerificationEmail = async (email) => {try {const token = getVerificationToken();const mailOptions = {from: "example@example.com", to: email, subject: "Email Verification", text: `Your verification token is: ${token}`};await transporter.sendMail(mailOptions);return token;} catch (error) {console.error(error);return null;}}app.post("/api/email/send-verification-email", async (req, res) => {if (await limiter.consume(req.body.email)) {const token = await sendVerificationEmail(req.body.email);if (token) {emails[req.body.email] = {token, verified: false};res.json({token});} else {res.status(500).json({error: "Failed to send verification email"})}} else {res.status(429).json({error: "Rate limit exceeded"})}});app.post("/api/email/verify-email", async (req, res) => {const email = req.body.email;const token = req.body.token;if (emails[email]) {if (emails[email].token === token) {emails[email].verified = true;res.json({verified: true});} else {res.status(401).json({error: "Invalid token"})}} else {res.status(404).json({error: "Email not found"})}});const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;app.listen(PORT, () => console.log('Server running on port ' + PORT));
